t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who's brimming with ambition. The golden thread brings tidings: They're <color=#dbc291ff>at the edge of a platform overlooking some fissures</color>, eager to take on a new challenge.

t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who has prepared a little surprise. The golden thread brings tidings: They're hiding <color=#dbc291ff>behind something fragile</color>, waiting to deliver their long-prepared opening line.

t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who delights in warmth. The golden thread brings tidings: They're nestling <color=#dbc291ff>beside that hero's embers</color>, savoring the lingering warmth of the flames.

t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who has been watching the scenery alone for ages. The golden thread brings tidings: They're still at the <color=#dbc291ff>edge of a distant height</color>, weaving that pink dream.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者沉浸在悲伤之中。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>世界的巨大创口前</color>，聆听记录亡者最后的声音呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is immersed in sorrow. The golden thread brings tidings: They're <color=#dbc291ff>in front of the vast wound of the world</color>, listening and recording the final cries of the dead.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者观看了你在这世界探索的全程。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>这趟旅程的终点</color>，期待见证你走向崭新的未来呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who has been watching your every step through this world. The golden thread brings tidings: They're waiting at <color=#dbc291ff>the end of this journey</color>, eager to witness you step into a bright new future.

t_ch: 嗯，我会一直在此等待。
t_en: It's alright. I'll wait here for you.

t_ch: 若虫到底是什么？
t_en: What exactly are the nymphs?

t_ch: 细小的疏漏，数据的赘生…我所不知的专业领域里，有许多描述这类存在的词汇。
t_en: Tiny oversights, redundant data... There are many terms for such beings in professional fields unknown to me.

t_ch: 造物主天衣无缝的演算中，本不该出现此等变量——但在每段旅程的终点，那位英雄都会向命运发起反抗，一遍又一遍……
t_en: In the Creator's flawless calculations, such variables were never meant to exist. Yet at the end of each journey, that hero would defy destiny, time and time again...

t_ch: 在此过程中，某些微小的变量得以诞生，这也是若虫的雏形。自那以后，若虫便成了每一个史诗故事的注脚。
t_en: During this process, certain minute variables came into being, which became the embryonic form of the nymphs. Since then, nymphs have become footnotes to every epic story.

t_ch: 那位救世主曾多次以若虫自比，若虫也确实因他而生。真是奇妙的命运啊。
t_en: That Deliverer often compared himself to a nymph, and indeed, nymphs came into being through him. What a fascinating twist of destiny.

t_ch: 「时光硕果」有什么用？
t_en: What's the use of the Fruit of Time?

t_ch: 在我有限的认知中，它是一艘「记忆」的航船。微小，却足以装载一些东西游到天幕之外去。
t_en: In my limited knowledge, it's an ark of the Remembrance. Small, yet capable of carrying certain things beyond the heavens.

t_ch: 活物太过复杂，不可能带走，但几件纪念品应该不成问题…或许，这才是阿格莱雅大人一直寻找的、真正战胜黑潮的方式。
t_en: Living beings are too complex to take away, but a few souvenirs shouldn't be a problem... Perhaps this is what Lady Aglaea has been searching for all along, the true way to defeat the black tide.

t_ch: 去问问那些你熟悉的朋友吧，也许他们有值得被你铭记之物。
t_en: Go, and ask the friends you know well. They might have something worth remembering.

t_ch: 有什么希望我铭记的东西吗？
t_en: Is there anything you want me to remember?

t_ch: 我们最深刻的共同记忆，莫过于和织者们发生的故事了。
t_en: Our deepest shared memory has to be the stories with the Weavers.

t_ch: 我曾用金丝为这些织者建造了一座乐园，如今它已变成「记忆」的一部分。希望你能收下。
t_en: I once built them a paradise with golden threads, and now it has become a part of the Remembrance. I hope you will accept it.

t_ch: 再会了。
t_en: Farewell.

t_ch: 嗯，我会一直在此等待。
t_en: It's alright. I'll wait here for you.

t_ch: 这里怎么有块石板…？
t_en: What's this stone tablet doing here...?

t_ch: （《粉霞天女历险记》？难道…我还在翁法罗斯？）
t_en: (Adventures of the Rosy Celestial Maiden? Could it be... am I still in Amphoreus?)

t_ch: （不对，感觉应该跟三月七有关系…还是问问她吧。）
t_en: (No, this must have something to do with March 7th... I should ask her about it.)

t_ch: 哎嘿嘿，上次来玩的时候偷偷放在这的，总算被你发现了。
t_en: Hehe~ I secretly left it here last time I came to play. You finally found it!

t_ch: 看来你还挺中意这称号？
t_en: Looks like you're quite fond of that title, huh?

t_ch: 当然啦！这名头可是本姑娘靠实力赚来的！
t_en: Of course! This title was earned by me, fair and square!

t_ch: 你居然能把它带出来？
t_en: You actually managed to bring it out?

t_ch: 没有没有，这是咱自己做的啦。
t_en: Nah, I made this myself!

t_ch: 这块石板只是个模型，里面没写一个字。不过，咱照着翁法罗斯的风格印了个封面，看着是不是有模有样？
t_en: This tablet's just a prop. There's not a single word written inside. But hey, I copied the Amphorean style for the cover. Looks pretty legit, don't you think?

t_ch: 还挺有纪念意义的。
t_en: That's quite memorable.

t_ch: 就是嘛，咱也陪了你们一路，结果一大堆照片里，却没几张能照到咱。明明还换了新造型，好可惜啊——
t_en: Right? I've been with you guys this whole time, but in all those photos, there's barely any of me in them. And I even got a new look. Such a waste!

t_ch: 苦了你了，三月七。
t_en: Sorry you had to go through that, March 7th.

t_ch: 我们的旅途还在继续，这就够啦！后面你们俩记得多请我喝几杯奶茶就行！
t_en: Our journey continues, and that's all that matters! Just remember to treat me to lots of bubble tea later, you two!

t_ch: 现在想想，还是有点遗憾，要是能从翁法罗斯多带点纪念品出来就好了。
t_en: Come to think of it, it's kind of a bummer. I wish I could've grabbed more souvenirs from Amphoreus.

t_ch: 哎呀，一旦开始多愁善感，就没完没了啦。总之，它就送给你啦，要记得摆在显眼的位置！
t_en: Aw geez, once I start getting all sentimental, there's no stopping me! Anyway, it's yours now. Make sure you put it somewhere everyone can see!

t_ch: 我猜你也有东西要送给我？
t_en: I'm guessing you've got something for me too?

t_ch: 没错，不过我在犹豫该怎么开口。
t_en: Indeed. Though I'm hesitant about how to bring it up.

t_ch: 怎么不说话？
t_en: Why aren't you talking?

t_ch: 我也有东西要交给你，但我在犹豫怎么开口。
t_en: I have something to give you as well, but I'm hesitating on how to bring it up.

t_ch: 三月前几天来找我，用合成机打印了石板和封面，那时候我就在构思了。
t_en: March came to see me a few days ago and used the Synthesizer to print out the tablet and cover. I was already thinking about it back then.

t_ch: 到底什么纪念物既能表达「开拓」，又契合你的风格——最后得出的结论就是它。
t_en: After considering what souvenir could represent both Trailblaze and match your style, this was the conclusion I reached.

t_ch: 收下吧，庆祝我们平安无事地走完了这一遭，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Take this. To celebrate that we made it through unscathed, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 三个人在一起才算完整，对吧？
t_en: Three's company, right?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 谢啦，丹恒。
t_en: Thanks, Dan Heng.

t_ch: 不客气，下次去偷夜宵时，记得你来打头阵。
t_en: Don't mention it. Next time we raid for midnight snacks, you'll take the frontline.

t_ch: 嗯，我想说的就是这些，开饭的时候见。
t_en: Mm, that's all I wanted to say. See you at mealtime.

t_ch: 至于这件礼物，如果觉得有些奇怪…不，没什么，希望你能中意。
t_en: As for this gift, if you find it a bit strange... No, never mind. I hope it's to your liking.

t_ch: 小心，不要触碰那块羽珀，否则「毁灭」的力量会将你一并浸染！
t_en: Watch out! Don't touch that feather-shaped amber, or the power of Destruction will consume you!

t_ch: 那位英雄用脱落的羽翼铸成了这道封印。若要靠近，得先解决{RUBY_B#「铁墓」}一切的源头{RUBY_E#}才行。
t_en: That hero forged this seal with their fallen wings. To get close, we must first deal with {RUBY_B#Irontomb}the source of everything{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 那位英雄终于拥抱了真我，迸发出耀眼的光芒……
t_en: That hero finally embraced their ego, erupting in brilliant radiance...

t_ch: 真是美丽的光芒啊，简直像世界的裂缝一样，我也想投身其中……
t_en: Such beautiful radiance... It's like a crack in the world itself. Even I want to dive right into it...

t_ch: 那道光芒…照射出了什么东西？
t_en: That light beam... what did it reveal?

t_ch: 「神话之外」？时光归墟里，竟然会有通往这地方的入口？
t_en: Exomyth? There's actually an entrance to that place in the Ruins of Time?

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}的内心生出一丝疑惑，却还是朝前方的屏幕走去。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} felt a hint of doubt, but still walked towards the screen ahead.

t_ch: 若虫们如灯泡般散发着忽明忽暗的光泽，怪热闹的。
t_en: The nymphs were glowing like lightbulbs, flickering on and off, quite a lively spectacle.

t_ch: 这里为什么会有若虫？或许是「权杖」不怕Bug吧。
t_en: Why are there nymphs here? Perhaps the Scepter is not afraid of bugs...

t_ch: 仔细一想，金属质地的若虫跟这里还蛮相配的。
t_en: Come to think of it, these metallic nymphs really fit in well around here.

t_ch: 这是一面古怪的屏幕。上次触碰它时，你不得不与那来古士战至天昏地暗。
t_en: This is one weird screen. Last time you touched it, you had to fight Lygus until all hell broke loose.

t_ch: 这次…什么也没发生。难道是缺了什么东西？
t_en: Nothing happened this time... Could something be missing?

t_ch: 这是一面古怪的屏幕。上次触碰它时，你不得不与那来古士战至天昏地暗……
t_en: This is one weird screen. Last time you touched it, you had to fight Lygus until all hell broke loose...

t_ch: 果然，行走于无名之途上的人，一定能发现这处巧思。不枉我将观众席的入口与那片空间相连。
t_en: As expected, those who walk the nameless path will surely discover this clever design. It wasn't in vain that I connected the audience entrance with that space.

t_ch: 来古士？
t_en: Lygus?

t_ch: 赞达尔？
t_en: Zandar?

t_ch: 你还活着？
t_en: You're still alive?

t_ch: 呵呵，别紧张，阁下——此刻于您眼前显现的，不过是系统底层的一串维护代码。
t_en: Heh heh, there's no need to be nervous. What you see before you is merely a string of maintenance code from the system's backend.

t_ch: 注销管理员权限之前，吕枯耳戈斯用了一点时间将我捏塑成型，并留下了一道谜题——
t_en: Before revoking Administrator privileges, Lycurgus took some time to mold me into shape, and left behind a riddle...

t_ch: 在这场实验中，真正导致失败的<color=#dbc291ff>变量</color>是什么？
t_en: In this experiment, what was the real <color=#dbc291ff>variable</color> that led to failure?

t_ch: 看来，你还没有找到自己的答案——时日无多，请尽快思索吧。
t_en: It seems you haven't found your answer yet. Time is running out, so please think it through quickly.

t_ch: 是啊，英雄——故事家会这样称呼他们，而我更愿称其为自由之人、充满无限可能性的人。
t_en: Yes, heroes. That's what storytellers call them, but I prefer to call them free spirits, people of infinite possibilities.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的七月名为自由月。它既是属于刻法勒的月份，亦是每名囚徒心底最渴望、却无法触及的东西。
t_en: July in Amphoreus is called the Month of Freedom. It belongs to Kephale and represents what every prisoner yearns for deep in their hearts, yet can never grasp.

t_ch: 人的精神是不可测算的，唯有神明能掌控一切。正如赞达尔无法剔除自身的人性，吕枯耳戈斯亦无法抹杀生命的出路。
t_en: The human spirit is immeasurable. Only the gods can control everything. Just as Zandar cannot remove his humanity, Lycurgus cannot eliminate the path of life.

t_ch: 于命途一题上，我们不过是命运的囚徒……
t_en: When it comes to the topic of Paths, we're nothing but prisoners of destiny...

t_ch: 但谁又能断言，我们不是一座更大洞穴中的囚徒呢？
t_en: But who can say for sure that we aren't just prisoners in an even bigger cave?

t_ch: 所以，我搜集了这些若虫，作为对自己和后来者的小小警示：「控制」不过是一种幻象。
t_en: So, I collected these nymphs as a small reminder to myself and those who come after: "Control" is nothing but an illusion.

t_ch: 行走在无名之途上的人啊，离别之前，请允许我为你献上一句箴言……
t_en: O wanderer on the nameless path, before we part ways, allow me to offer you these words of wisdom...

t_ch: 你是一块绝好的石料，勿要放弃这份可塑性——它是你身上最大的卓越。
t_en: You're a diamond in the rough. Don't lose that malleability, for it is your greatest excellence.

t_ch: 直到你决定踏出这片<color=#dbc291ff>{RUBY_B#世界}洞穴{RUBY_E#}</color>之前……
t_en: Until you decide to step out of this <color=#dbc291ff>{RUBY_B#world}cave{RUBY_E#}</color>...

t_ch: 流动的光路，很像金线呢~
t_en: The flowing light path really looks like a golden thread~

t_ch: 终于来到这里了喀~
t_en: Finally here, crrk~

t_ch: 谢谢你让我们回家。
t_en: Thank you for letting us get home.

t_ch: 到了那时，就去找衣匠吧。
t_en: When the time comes, go find the Garmentmaker.

t_ch: 花苞随着某种脉搏的节律鼓动，猩红色孢子随之喷涌而出。
t_en: The flower bud pulsates with a certain rhythmic beat. Following that, crimson spores gush forth.

t_ch: 你曾听说繁育沾染的星球，孢种甚至能在旅者的鼻壁上生根发芽。为了你的鼻腔健康，或许不应该离它太近。
t_en: You've heard about planets tainted by the Propagation, where spores can even take root in travelers' nasal cavities. For the sake of your olfactory health, you probably shouldn't get too close.

t_ch: 先是一股浓腻的香气，随后是花蕊兴奋的颤舞…似乎嗅到了你身上熟悉的危险气息，花蕊突然停顿，瑟缩回花苞之中。
t_en: First came a thick, cloying fragrance, followed by an exhilarated dance of the stamen... Seemingly detecting the familiar-yet-dangerous aura about you, the flower's stamen suddenly paused, shrinking back into its bud.

t_ch: 被蛀空的星空，沦为虫群孵育子嗣的卵床…梦境向你展示了一段残酷的回忆。
t_en: The hollowed-out starscape, reduced to a breeding ground for the Swarm's offsprings... The dreamscape revealed a cruel memory to you.

t_ch: 真蛰虫肆虐的年代，虫群分泌粘液连接被侵蚀殆尽的星体，织就一张又一张连亘数光年的巨网。
t_en: In the era ravaged by True Sting, the Swarm secreted mucus connecting corroded celestial bodies, weaving massive nets spanning light-years across space.

t_ch: 对于曾眺望过这番景象的那些世界而言，能做的只有祈祷和等待。
t_en: For worlds that had witnessed this sight, all they could do was pray and wait.

t_ch: 被蛀空的星空，沦为虫群孵育子嗣的卵床…梦境向你展示了一段残酷的回忆。
t_en: The hollowed-out starscape, reduced to a breeding ground for the Swarm's offsprings... The dreamscape revealed a cruel memory to you.

t_ch: 太一之手撕裂了夜空， 虫卵破溃，繁育的力量喷涌而出。
t_en: The hands of Ena lacerated the night sky, as insect eggs burst open, unleashing the power of Propagation.

t_ch: 匮乏。仅仅远望一眼，你便感到无比匮乏。你的脊椎开始思考，口器吱咯作响，渴望用一场血肉的盛宴填满空若无物的胸腔…你最好把视线移开。
t_en: Scarcity. Just one distant glance, and you feel the overwhelming scarcity. Your spine begins to think, your mouthparts clicking and grinding, yearning to fill the empty void in your chest with a feast of flesh and blood... You'd better look away.

t_ch: 受繁育沾染的巨大蕈菌，想必一次可以吃很久。
t_en: This massive mushroom, tainted by the Propagation, could probably provide sustenance for ages.

t_ch: 再以这样的势头生长下去，这些蕈菌或许也能发展出璀璨的文明，在某个幽暗的地域演绎一出争夺王位的戏码。
t_en: At this rate of growth, these fungi might just develop their own dazzling civilization and put on a show of power struggles for the throne in some dark corner of the universe.

t_ch: 本着实事求是的探究精神，你摸了摸蕈菌上附着的织体。柔软，黏糊，富有弹性，还有点火辣辣的。你得出结论：下次不要摸了。
t_en: In the spirit of scientific curiosity, you touched the tissue attached to the fungus. Soft, slimy, elastic, and a bit spicy-hot. Your conclusion: don't touch it next time.

t_ch: 这颗「死灭之茧」曾捏造出一张悬垂的丝网，如愿诱捕到了寻觅而至的飞萤。
t_en: This "Cocoon of Extinction" had woven a suspended web of silk, successfully luring and trapping the fyreflies that came searching.

t_ch: 同为盛放星核的容器，你简直与它们油然而生了惺惺相惜之情。
t_en: Being a fellow vessel for Stellarons, you can't help but feel a kindred spirit with them.

t_ch: 阴谋与论辩、创造与死亡，以及抵押生命也要完成的宏伟志愿…你仿佛才刚亲眼目睹，但已是前尘旧事。
t_en: Conspiracies and disputations, creation and death, and grand aspirations worth betting one's life on... Though it feels like you just witnessed it all, it's already become a tale of the past.

t_ch: 帷幕之后隐隐浮现白漆绘制的图案。三个同心圆，构成了橡木横截面上的年轮，你隐约记起这是橡木家系的家徽。
t_en: Behind the curtains, white-painted images gradually emerge. Three concentric circles form the growth rings on an oak cross-section. You vaguely recall this as the Oak Family's insignia.

t_ch: 橡木家系的家徽。橡木年轮上的三个缺口，究竟是对希佩三重面相的礼赞，还是在暗喻寄居木中的蛀虫？
t_en: The Oak Family's insignia. Are the three notches in the oak's growth rings a tribute to the Triple-Faced Soul, or do they hint at the woodworms dwelling within?

t_ch: 在家徽上画一株被拦腰折断的橡木，会不会有点不大吉利？你真替橡木家系感到担心。
t_en: Drawing a snapped oak tree on the family insignia... that's pretty inauspicious, isn't it? You're genuinely worried about the Oak Family.

t_ch: 古旧的轮椅，在道路的尽头像一座墓碑矗立。
t_en: An old wheelchair stands like a tombstone at the end of the road.

t_ch: 书籍、钢笔和纸张依旧停留在书桌上，但再也等不到它们的主人。
t_en: The books, pens, and papers remain on the desk, but they'll never see their owner again.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼命运的众多分岔路中，这一条在彼时走到尽头…再往前是一片望不到底的虚空。
t_en: Among the many branching paths of Penacony's destiny, this one has reached its end... with nothing but an endless void lying ahead.

t_ch: 自动钢琴被猩红色的荒草吞没，琴键残缺，零星、生涩地发出走调的单音。
t_en: The automatic piano was engulfed by crimson-colored wild grass. Its keys were damaged and missing, sporadically and stiffly emitting discordant single notes.

t_ch: 你从未见过如此…丰腴的蝴蝶，你不禁联想起饱饮鲜血的蚊子，有着鼓鼓囊囊的腹部。
t_en: You've never seen such a... plump butterfly before. You can't help but think of a blood-gorged mosquito with a swollen belly.

t_ch: 「繁育」的力量流溢。每一根枝条、每一片叶片都因紧绷而微微翕动，迫不及待挣脱束缚自己的牢笼。
t_en: The power of Propagation overflows. Every branch and leaf quivers with tension, desperately yearning to break free from their cage's confinement.

t_ch: 时钟的点缀，书籍排列构成的线条，乃至书脊上每一个烫金的字体，仿佛都冥冥中服膺于一个伟大且和谐的比例。
t_en: The ornate clocks, the lines formed by organized books, and even every gilded letter on their spines. It all seemed to mystically conform to some grand and harmonious proportion.

t_ch: 一切都在自己恰如其分的位置上，简单、洁净、井井有条。
t_en: Everything is in its proper place: simple, pristine, and perfectly organized.

t_ch: 「同谐」的力量流溢。最单纯的满足温热地上升，填满你的胸膛。再在这里呆下去，你恐怕会幸福得昏厥过去。
t_en: The power of Harmony overflows. The purest satisfaction rises warmly, filling your chest. If you stay here any longer, you might just pass out from sheer bliss.

t_ch: 似有若无的香气悬浮，盘旋之处绵密得堪称结实，空隙之处又无比稀疏。你置身的这片空间似乎拥有不均的质地。
t_en: A faint fragrance lingers in the air, dense enough to feel almost solid where it swirls, yet incredibly sparse in the gaps. The space in which you find yourself seems to have an uneven texture.

t_ch: 滴…嗒，滴答，滴——答，时钟也以不一致的步伐掠过刻度。你的目光划过这处神秘的所在。
t_en: Tick... tock, tick-tock, tick—tock, the clock marches past its marks with uneven steps. You glanced across the entirety of this mysterious place.

t_ch: 幽绿色的药剂在昏暗中散发荧光，不知道口感如何。
t_en: The pale green potion glows in the darkness. You wonder how it tastes like.

t_ch: 书桌的一角的各式年鉴、典籍、杂志下，压着各不相同，甚至自相矛盾的身份证明。
t_en: Pinned beneath various almanacs, classic volumes, and magazines in a corner of the desk were multiple identification documents. All different, and some even self-contradictory.

t_ch: 一面碎镜，停留在了破裂的一刻。对碎镜的钟爱，好像是忆者的出厂设置。
t_en: A shattered mirror, frozen at the moment of breaking. The love for broken mirrors seems to be the default factory setting for Memokeepers.

t_ch: 高背椅微妙的弧度能够完美地承托背部，暗示着它的主人有着良好品味，且不是一个能够忍受正襟危坐的人。
t_en: The subtle curve of the high-back chair perfectly supports the back, suggesting that its owner has impeccable taste, and is not one to tolerate improper behavior.

t_ch: 你们几个烂西瓜！既然已经决定改邪归正！会一门生存技能是非常重要的。拿出以前做坏事的脑子！给我好好学习啊！
t_en: You rotten watermelons! Since you've already decided to turn over a new leaf, it's incredibly important to learn a craft or two to survive. If you've got the brains to pick up crimes, use it to learn instead!

t_ch: （听听他们在说什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 我们这帮粗人，哪里懂得怎么搞家政服务啊？又要打扫卫生，又要微笑服务…这我们真搞不定啊！大姐头！
t_en: What do bumpkins like us know about housekeeping service? You gotta clean and serve with a smile... We really can't do it, boss!

t_ch: 你个烂西瓜！谁让你叫大姐头了，说了多少次都记不住？我们正经人的世界存在「大姐头」吗？要叫海梅斯老师！
t_en: You rotten watermelon! Who told you to call me lady boss? How many times must I say it!? There are no lady bosses among decent folk. Call me Ms. Hymes!

t_ch: 对不起！海梅斯老师！我立刻自刎归天以谢天下！
t_en: I'm sorry! Ms. Hymes! Allow me to pay with my life!

t_ch: 停！你们现在都是正经人了！不要动不动就自刎归天！先来复习一下上节课学的内容…照看人类婴儿的时候，婴儿突然哭了起来，你们应该怎么办？
t_en: Stop! You're all civilized individuals now. Stop thinking about paying with lives! Let's review our previous lesson... When taking care of a human infant and the infant suddenly starts crying, what should you do?

t_ch: 给孩子讲述我们当年和永火官邸火并的故事。当时我身中数枪，一滴眼泪都没有流！因为老大说过，「泯灭帮永不哭泣」！
t_en: Tell the child about our shootout with the Ever-Flame Mansion back in the day. I took multiple bullets, but I didn't shed a single tear! Because our boss said, "Annihilation Gang never cries!"

t_ch: 住口，你这个暴力烂西瓜！给我写<unbreak>400</unbreak>字检讨反省一下…但是不许自刎归天！
t_en: Shut up, you violent rotten watermelon! Write a <unbreak>400</unbreak>-word reflective essay... but none of you are allowed to pay with your lives!

t_ch: 来，旁边那个看起来很认真的烂西瓜，你来回答一下！
t_en: Now, that serious-looking rotten watermelon over there! You answer this question!

t_ch: 我会轻轻抱起婴儿，塞入安抚奶嘴，缓慢地摇晃。
t_en: I will gently carry the infant, give them a pacifier, and rock them softly.

t_ch: 嗯，你这西瓜还不错，说的还算句人话。
t_en: Hmm, you're not too bad. A decent answer, at least.

t_ch: 然后我会在婴儿的耳边轻声说：「如果您再这么哭，下次来找您的就是反物质军团了。」
t_en: Then I'll whisper to the infant: "If you keep crying like this, next time it'll be the Antimatter Legion coming for you."

t_ch: 你这臭放贷眼镜烂西瓜！威胁婴儿的态度还挺恭敬是吧？你再敢把以前收高利贷的那副嘴脸拿出来，下次来找你的就是你的假释监督员了！
t_en: You four-eyed, loan-sharking piece of rotten watermelon! Quite the respectful tone you've got there for threatening a baby. You try that debt collector act one more time, and the next person knocking on your door will be your parole officer!

t_ch: 对不起！海梅斯老师！我立刻自刎归天以谢天下！
t_en: I'm sorry! Ms. Hymes! Allow me to pay with my life!

t_ch: 不许刎！！！
t_en: Don't you dare!!!

t_ch: （先走了）
t_en: (Leave)

t_ch: 你们这群烂西瓜，不要以为「改邪归正」是一件简单的事。做过错事的人，想要过上普通人的生活，必须付出巨大的代价才行！听懂了吗！
t_en: You bunch of rotten watermelons, don't think that it's easy to "turn over a new leaf." People who've done wrong and want to live a normal life must pay a huge cost! You hear me!?

t_ch: 咦？这不是星穹列车的客人吗？你怎么一个人跑到鸽川区来了？这里的治安不太好，一定要注意安全。有什么需要我们帮助的吗？
t_en: Huh? Aren't you a guest from the Astral Express? What are you doing out here in Dovebrook District all by yourself? The security here isn't great, so you need to be careful. Is there anything we can do for you?

t_ch: 你们是治安官吗？
t_en: Are you security officers?

t_ch: 没错。我们两个是专门负责鸽川区治安工作的治安官。我是古斯，这位则是部长卡拉。
t_en: That's right. The two of us are officers in charge of security for Dovebrook District. I'm Gus, and this is Chief Carla.

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 狗的官职比你高？
t_en: The dog outranks you?

t_ch: 什么「狗」啊…卡拉部长是「犬灵」！是异常防御部认定的幻造种公民，也是一位在这条街上德高望重的老治安官！
t_en: What do you mean, "dog"? Chief Carla is a "woofae"! A registered imagenae citizen recognized by the Department of Aberration Defense, and a highly respected veteran security officer on this street!

t_ch: 这是你的治安犬吗？
t_en: Is this your security dog?

t_ch: 什么「治安犬」啊…卡拉部长是「犬灵」！是异常防御部认定的幻造种公民，也是一位在这条街上德高望重的老治安官！
t_en: What do you mean, "security dog"? Chief Carla is a woofae! A registered imagenae citizen recognized by the Department of Aberration Defense, and a highly respected veteran security officer on this street!

t_ch: 汪…呜汪汪！汪！
t_en: Woof... Rrr—ruff ruff! Woof!

t_ch: 唉…您说的对。域外人士确实容易因为外表而产生混淆，不能因此责怪别人…抱歉，无名客，刚才是我的态度不好。
t_en: *sigh* You have a point. It's easy for outworlders to get confused by appearances. I shouldn't hold it against them... My apologies, Nameless. I was out of line.

t_ch: 汪汪汪，汪汪！
t_en: Rrr—ruff ruff, woof!

t_ch: 部长说，鸽川区本来就是个治安混乱的地方，幻月游戏期间更是雪上加霜，你们一定不要惹上纠纷！
t_en: The Chief says that Dovebrook District is a lawless place to begin with, and things get even worse during the Phantasmoon Games. You need to make sure you don't get into any disputes!

t_ch: 汪！呜呜！汪！
t_en: Woof! Wuoof! Woof!

t_ch: 部长说，如果你卷入了什么纠纷，请第一时间联系我们，不要自己着手解决…哎呀，部长，人家是无名客，战斗力很强的，用不着我们操心吧？
t_en: The Chief says that if you get caught up in any trouble, please contact us first and foremost. Don't try to resolve it on your own... But chief, this person is one of the Nameless. They're very strong. I don't think we need to be concerned, do we?

t_ch: 呜呜…汪！汪呜！
t_en: Wuoof... Woof! Arf-woof!

t_ch: 啊，您说的对…部长的意思是，正因为你战斗力太强了，所以才请你尽可能不要自己解决纠纷。
t_en: Oh, you have a point... The Chief means that it's precisely because you're so strong that he's asking you not to resolve these matters personally.

t_ch: 你要是在鸽川区把哪个不长眼的小混混给打伤打残了…那可真是不好收场。
t_en: If you were to seriously injure or maim some thug who picked a fight with you in Dovebrook District... that would be a very difficult situation to handle.

t_ch: 汪汪——汪！
t_en: Rrr—ruff ruff... Woof!

t_ch: 总而言之，请一定要注意安全！有什么问题随时来找我们！
t_en: Anyway, just stay safe! And don't hesitate to find us if you need anything!

t_ch: 我就是路过，这就走。
t_en: I was just passing by. I'll be on my way now.

t_ch: 了解了。你自己注意安全，有什么问题随时来找我们！
t_en: I understand. Please be safe, and don't hesitate to find us if you need anything!

t_ch: 经理…你这混蛋上司…为了陪你喝麦芽汁，老子又没赶上末班车！可恶啊…又要在外面露宿了啊！
t_en: Oh Manager... you jerk... Had to drink that malt juice with you and missed the last train again! Darn it... Guess I'm sleeping on the streets tonight!

t_ch: 我赶时间，先走了。
t_en: I'm in a hurry, see you around.

t_ch: 好的呢{NICKNAME}老板辛苦了但是再苦再累都要记得好好吃饭不然身体会吃不消的毕竟人是铁饭是钢一顿不吃饿得慌说起来我一直觉得这句谚语很奇怪到底为什么饭是钢听起来很硌牙呢！
t_en: Sure thing Boss! I know you're working hard but no matter how tired or exhausted you are remember to eat properly after all your body won't be able to keep up otherwise and you know how they say your body needs fuel to run properly! Though I've always found that saying weird since people don't drink gasoline and can't burn it anyway so why would they need to consume fuel for energy?

t_ch: 喂，这位粉丝！请你和我保持距离，尤其不要给我拍照。如果暴露了我的行踪，整条街都会被粉丝们挤爆的。请多多包含。
t_en: Hey there, fan! Please give me some space, and absolutely no photos. If you blow my cover, this entire street will be mobbed by my fans. So, if you'd be so kind.

t_ch: 请问你是？
t_en: And you are...?

t_ch: 别装了。你这身衣服不便宜吧？这说明你就像我一样，是个体面人。而你我这种体面人怎么会出现在鸽川区？一定是有所求。
t_en: Oh, stop the act. That's a nice outfit you've got on. It tells me you're a person of refinement, just like myself. So why would refined people like us be in a place like the Dovebrook District? We must be here on business.

t_ch: 现在既然你出现在我面前，那我就可以断定——你是来见我的。
t_en: The fact that you're standing before me now can only mean one thing: You've come to see me.

t_ch: 至于你是从哪里得知了我的行踪…大概是从社交网站上找到的吧？真拿你们这些狂热粉丝没办法啊。呵，看你那惊异的表情，没想到我能看穿真相吧？
t_en: And how did you learn of my whereabouts? Let me guess... social media? Honestly, you rabid fans are something else. Hehe, that shocked expression... You never thought I'd see through you, did you?

t_ch: 你可别小看我，我可是推理剧专业户！《乐园疑云》《神探下了课》《尘灵猎人》《超动感警探》…既然你是我的粉丝，那应该都看过吧？
t_en: Don't you underestimate me. I'm a specialist in mystery shows! Arcadia Mystery, After Class Detective, Wispae Hunter, The Hyper-Mentalist... Since you're such a big fan, you must have seen them all, yes?

t_ch: 那你为啥跑到鸽川区？
t_en: So what brings you to the Dovebrook District?

t_ch: 啧…都被你找到这里来了，那我大概也算是瞒不过去了…我现在的热度越来越低了，为了维持愿力，只能来鸽川区寻求些「专业手段」了。
t_en: Tch... Well, you've found me, so I guess the secret's out... My popularity is fading. To keep my Wishpower from drying up, I had to come to the Dovebrook District to seek out some... "specialized services."

t_ch: 说穿了，就是来这里找些专业人士在网上给我造造势。我也不知道他们都怎么干…但方法无所谓，有愿力汇入就好。
t_en: Basically, I'm looking for some experts to manufacture some buzz for me online. I don't know the details of what they do... The methods don't matter, as long as the Wishpower keeps coming.

t_ch: 别这么看着我嘛！你也知道的，现在大环境不好，并不是我的问题。
t_en: Oh, don't look at me like that! It's a tough market out there, you know? It's not like I have a choice.

t_ch: 没看过。你演的是侦探？
t_en: Haven't seen them. Are you a detective in those?

t_ch: 呵，跟我玩这种欲擒故纵的小把戏是吧？你都追到这里来了，那又怎么会不记得我演过什么呢？
t_en: Hehe, trying that old "playing hard to get" trick on me, eh? You chased me all the way to this place, so there's no way you don't remember what I've acted in.

t_ch: 《乐园疑云》第三季第五集的死者、《神探下了课》第十九季第五集的死者、《尘灵猎人》第五季第五集的死者…我演过的死者可太多了！
t_en: The corpse in Season 3, Episode 5 of Arcadia Mystery, the corpse in Season 19, Episode 5 of After Class Detective, the corpse in Season 5, Episode 5 of Wispae Hunter... I've played so many corpses!

t_ch: 最厉害的还是《超动感警探》，在第七集里面，我一个人扮演了五个死者！你肯定还对我当时的演技记忆犹新吧？
t_en: But my best work was in The Hyper-Mentalist. In Episode 7, I single-handedly played five different dead bodies! Surely you remember my breathtaking performance?

t_ch: 行了，能和偶像聊这么久，你也应该满意了吧？快别在这儿缠着我了，要是让狗仔队拍到我在鸽川区和粉丝密会，谁知道他们会乱写些什么报道！
t_en: Alright, you've had a nice long chat with your idol, you should be satisfied. Now stop hanging around me. If the paparazzi catch me having a clandestine meeting with a fan in Dovebrook District, there's no telling what kind of nonsense they'll write!

t_ch: （当没看见好了）
t_en: (I'll just act like I never saw him.)

t_ch: 等等，你别急着走啊！只要你保证不拿出去倒卖，我也是可以答应和你合影的呀！签名也不是不能商量的呀！
t_en: Hey, wait, don't just walk off! As long as you promise not to resell it, I could even agree to a photo op! An autograph might be negotiable, too!

t_ch: 嗯？哦，你好…有什么事需要帮助吗？虽然人生最多还有三万多天，但如你所见，我现在并没有什么急事，所以你如果有什么困难就告诉我吧…我会在力所能及的范围内伸出援手哦。
t_en: Hm? Oh, hello... Need any help? Even though I've got about <unbreak>30,000</unbreak> days left in life, as you can see, I'm not particularly busy right now, so if you're having any troubles, just let me know... I'll lend a hand where I can.

t_ch: 没什么，随便聊聊。
t_en: Nothing much, just wanted to chat.

t_ch: 好的，随便聊聊。
t_en: Alright, let's just chat.

t_ch: 唔，糟糕，我在力所能及的范围内想不出来什么话题耶…怎么办，要写一封血书向您谢罪吗？哎呀——别紧张，只是一个玩笑而已。如果你真的需要，我会用红墨水认真写的。
t_en: Hmm, this is bad, I can't think of any topics... What should I do, write you an apology letter in blood? Oh, don't worry, just kidding. Though if you really need one, I'll write it properly with red ink.

t_ch: 我好像迷路了。
t_en: I think I'm lost.

t_ch: 诶，迷路？…哦，难道您没有在手机上安装《星际和平地图》这款应用？
t_en: Eh, lost? ...Oh, don't tell me you haven't installed the Interstellar Peace Map app on your phone?

t_ch: 很简单的，从应用市场里找到这款地图，安装一下，手机就可以提供移动导航的服务啦。
t_en: It's super simple... Just find this map app in the app store, install it, and your phone will provide mobile navigation services!

t_ch: 我忘带零钱了，能帮帮我吗？
t_en: I forgot my money. Could you help me out?

t_ch: 哎呀，那可太糟糕了。现在移动支付这么流行，碰到那种老式的投币售货机就容易让人毫无办法呢。
t_en: Oh man, that's a real bummer. With mobile payments being so popular these days, running into those old-school coin-operated vending machines can really leave you high and dry.

t_ch: 这<unbreak>100</unbreak>信用点里凝结着我的生命和汗水…我就把它托付给您了。请一定要好好使用它啊。
t_en: These <unbreak>100</unbreak> credits contain my life and sweat... I'm entrusting it to you. Please make good use of it.

t_ch: 打扰了，只是路过而已。
t_en: Sorry to bother you. I'm just passing through.

t_ch: 哦，抱歉…是我误会了…不好意思。有空再聊天吧…嗯，希望我们还有见面的机会。
t_en: Oh, sorry... I misunderstood... my bad. Let's chat again sometime. I hope we'll meet again.

t_ch: …啊！突然想起来。人们常说，相逢就是缘——
t_en: ...Ah! Just remembered. You know what they say: every encounter is unique and destined...

t_ch: 所以，还有什么我能够帮到你的吗？人们都说成为朋友的关键，就是先相互伸出援手！
t_en: So, is there anything else I can help you with? Everyone says that the key to becoming friends is to help each other first!

t_ch: 相逢就是缘。有什么我能够帮到你的吗？
t_en: Every encounter is unique and destined. How may I help you today?

t_ch: 夸我两句吧。
t_en: Come on, give me some compliments.

t_ch: 唔…你身上有一种特别的魅力，总是能吸引大家的注意？你的笑容像阳光一样温暖，一定能照亮整个世界？不管遇到什么困难，你总能迎难而上解决问题，是一个值得信赖的朋友？
t_en: Hmm... You have this special charm that always draws everyone's attention? Your smile is as warm as sunshine, surely brightening up the whole world? No matter what challenges come your way, you always rise to the occasion and solve problems. A truly reliable friend, right?

t_ch: 啊…我好像不是非常擅长这个。在人生的前一万天里很少做这种事情。如果效果不好，请接受我的道歉。
t_en: Ah... I don't think I'm particularly good at this. Haven't done much of this sort of thing in the first ten thousand days of my life. If it doesn't turn out well, please accept my apologies.

t_ch: 为什么戴着口罩？
t_en: Why are you wearing a mask?

t_ch: …咦？好奇这个吗？唔，应该说是节省生命能量的必要举措吧。
t_en: ...Huh? Curious about that? Well, let's just say it's a necessary measure to conserve life energy.

t_ch: 不化妆会不好意思出门，但是全脸化妆又非常麻烦…戴口罩的话，只要画好眼睛就可以啦。嗯，其实就是懒得化妆。
t_en: I'd feel self-conscious going out without makeup, but doing a full face is such a hassle... With a face mask on, I just need to do my eyes and I'm good to go. Well, I guess I'm just too lazy to make up.

t_ch: 唔，这么说来还是上班的时候轻松。我的工作属于难得的上班不用化妆的那种。不过缺点就是…上班的时候也不好穿表达个性的衣服。
t_en: Hmm, come to think of it, work is pretty chill. My job's one of those rare ones where I don't have to put on makeup. Though the downside is... I can't really wear clothes that express my personality at work.

t_ch: 所以你的职业是…？
t_en: So, what's your profession...?

t_ch: 啊…我家是开旧书店的。在店里要和各种各样的书打交道，也有一些上了年纪的客户，所以穿着打扮都要尽量朴素一些。
t_en: Ah... My family runs a used bookstore. Since I deal with all kinds of books at the shop and we have quite a few elderly customers, I try to keep my appearance fairly modest.

t_ch: 店面本身不大，就开在我们家老房子的一楼…来的客人也都很安静，所以有种像古墓一样令人安心的感觉。哎呀…这样想来，这份从爷爷手里继承下来的工作确实挺不错的。
t_en: The shop itself isn't big, just on the ground floor of our old house... The customers are always quiet, giving it this weirdly comforting tomb-like vibe. Heh... Come to think of it, this job I inherited from grandpa isn't half bad.

t_ch: 比老爸老妈那样的标准上班族还是舒服多啦。
t_en: Way better than being a regular office worker like Mom and Dad.

t_ch: （结束这个话题）
t_en: (End the subject)

t_ch: 旁边的那本书是幻造种吗？
t_en: Is that book next to you an imagenae?

t_ch: 哎呀…阁下真是慧眼如炬。
t_en: Oh my... Your Excellency is truly perceptive.

t_ch: 没错，那本立体书也是幻造种…不过由于愿力微薄，它的身体已经开始分崩离析，上面的立体插图早已消失不见…甚至糟糕到了不是所有人都能看见它本身的地步。
t_en: That's right, that pop-up book is also an imagenae... but due to its weak Wishpower, its body has already started to fall apart. The pop-up illustrations have long since vanished... it's even gotten so bad that not everyone can see the book itself anymore.

t_ch: 如果阁下也有拯救它的意愿，还请多多留心身边的环境：<color=#dbc291ff>这本书上已经分崩离析的立体插图，可能就隐藏在世界的角落之中</color>。
t_en: If you're also willing to help save it, please pay close attention to your surroundings: <color=#dbc291ff>the shattered pop-up illustrations from this book might be hidden in corners of the world</color>.

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 请多保重。希望下次还有机会。
t_en: Take care. Hope we'll meet again.

t_ch: 怎么一天又过去了！唔，我看看…腿断过、肠胃炎犯过、感冒发烧咳嗽早得过了…
t_en: How did another day just fly by! Hmm, let me see... broken legs, gastroenteritis, cold, fever, and cough... Cliche excuses...

t_ch: 哎，到底还有什么理由可以跟老师请假啊！
t_en: Ugh, what excuse can I possibly give to the teacher this time!

t_ch: 你是学生？为什么不上学？
t_en: You're a student? Why aren't you in school?

t_ch: 我在上学啊，谁说「装上学」不是一种上学？这可不比坐在高中教室轻松！
t_en: I AM going to school! Who says "pretending to go to school" isn't a form of schooling? It's not any easier than sitting in a high school classroom!

t_ch: 我每天都得起个大早，跟爸妈说「我上学去啦！」，接着再给老师发小作文：「非常抱歉妮卡又得请假了」，然后坐三站列车溜到这里来找有为姐姐。
t_en: Every morning I have to get up super early, tell my parents "I'm off to school!", then send my teacher a little note saying "I deeply apologize but Nikka needs to take another day off," and hop on the carriage for three stops to come see Miss Willyn.

t_ch: 你为什么不想上学？
t_en: Why don't you want to go to school?

t_ch: 好奇怪的问题，难道你想上班？
t_en: What a weird question! Do you actually want to go to work?

t_ch: 好啦，我知道你的意思。逃学是我自己的决定，我知道这不对，但除了你和有为姐姐，没人会问我为什么，大人们只会说——
t_en: Alright, I get what you mean. Skipping school was my own decision, I know it's wrong, but besides you and Willyn, no one asks me why. Adults just say...

t_ch: 「话别讲这么难听，那不是孤立，是你自己不合群！」，「起外号？要有欢愉精神啊妮卡，你就是太敏感！」，「都是孩子，什么打不打，闹着玩而已」……
t_en: "Don't make it sound so harsh, this isn't isolation. It's just that you don't fit in!", "Nicknames? Come on Nikka, where's your spirit of Elation? You're way too sensitive," "Kids fight all the time! It's not bullying, just harmless fun..."

t_ch: 就是这样。所以…我暂时不想上学。
t_en: And that's that. So... I don't want to go to school for now.

t_ch: 「有为姐姐」是谁？
t_en: Who is "Miss Willyn"?

t_ch: 有为姐姐是我的好朋友！喏，就是那个强壮的欧克姐姐！
t_en: Miss Willyn is my good friend! Look, she's that strong orch lady over there!

t_ch: 以前的我，生活给我一拳，我一躺就是一天！现在的我，每天都想成为她那样的世间强者！
t_en: When life threw a punch, I used to just lie there. Now, I wake up every day striving to be like her, who's strong enough to take on the world!

t_ch: 我计划找她锤炼自己，等我也变得强大了，我就回学校去，然后把那些欺负过我的人都揪出来，说：「——我要一次打八个！」
t_en: I'm gonna ask her to train me. Once I'm strong enough, I'll go back to school and line up those bullies, one by one, and say: "Come at me! I can take all eight of you at once!"

t_ch: 我也想不出，先溜了。
t_en: Can't figure it out either. I'm out.

t_ch: 没事，想不出理由我就直接开摆。俗话说得好——只要胆子大，一周七天假～
t_en: No worries. When words fail me, I just... stop trying. As they say: with guts comes freedom, every day's a holiday!

t_ch: 啊啊！欧克为什么就非要做打打杀杀的营生！哪有这样的道理！
t_en: Argh! Why do orch always have to make a living through violence and bloodshed!? Who made up that rule!?

t_ch: 哎呀，抱歉吓到你了！世界如此美妙，我却如此暴躁，这样不好…不好……
t_en: Ah, sorry! Didn't mean to scare you. The world is so wonderful, yet I'm so grumpy here... not good... not good at all.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 多谢您的关心，我没事，我只是再次求职失败罢了…没事的，我已经习惯了。
t_en: Oh, thank you for asking. I'm fine, really. Just... failed another job interview. Again. But I'm used to it by now.

t_ch: 为什么所有用人单位都在问我「擅不擅长战斗」「要不要转岗安保部门」「会不会忍不住殴打客户」这种蠢问题啊？我看起来难道是个很暴力的人吗！
t_en: Why do all these recruiters keep asking me stupid questions like "Are you good at combat," "Would you like to transfer to security," "Ever worry you'll punch a client?" Do I really look that violent to you!?

t_ch: 哎…我们欧克自世人对暴力的恐惧和羡慕中诞生，也把这样的印象留给了后人。所以直到今天，欧克的就业之路也很狭窄。
t_en: ...We orch were born from humanity's fear and admiration of violence, and that impression has been passed down through generations. That's why even today, career options for orch remain quite limited.

t_ch: 大家似乎很难信任欧克会真正放下他们所擅长的暴力，所以我爸妈常说：「只有去做『治安官』，才能被人打心底里尊重」。
t_en: It seems people find it hard to trust that we orch can truly abandon the violence we excel in. That's why my parents always say, "Only by becoming a security officer, can you earn true respect."

t_ch: 我当然尊敬治安官的牺牲，但厌烦暴力的我更想突破大家对欧克的认知，尝试一些不必使用武力的和平岗位。
t_en: I do respect security officers and all their sacrifices. However, as one who is tired of violence, I want to break through everyone's stereotypes about orch and try peaceful jobs that don't rely on force.

t_ch: 可现实却…很不顺利，外加爸妈催得紧，我只能假装上班，躲在这投简历。
t_en: Reality, though... hasn't been kind. With my parents breathing down my neck, all I can do is pretend to work while hiding here sending out resumes.

t_ch: 不过往好处想，至少旁边那位妮卡妹妹还很需要我…需要我教她怎么打架。哎呀烦死了！怎么所有人都觉得我是武力派呢？我明明是头脑派！大学时学的可是生化电子学喔！
t_en: Looking on the bright side though, at least little Nikka still needs me... needs me to teach her how to fight. Ugh, honestly! Why does everyone assume I'm the muscle type? I'm totally the brainy type! I majored in biochemical electronics in university, you know?

t_ch: 不过嘛，如果最终能帮到妮卡妹妹的话，那至少说明「武力」也不算全无用处吧。哈哈。
t_en: But I guess... if my fists can help Nikka even a little, maybe might isn't entirely useless after all. Haha.

t_ch: 没事，我先走了。
t_en: Well, I'm heading off, then.

t_ch: 吓到你真的很抱歉！我之后会努力保持情绪稳定，做最和平的欧克！
t_en: I'm so sorry for scaring you! I'll do my best to stay calm from now on... to be the most peace-loving orch there ever was!

t_ch: 章鱼，还是鱿鱼？静下心想想，我的胃现在渴望什么……
t_en: Octopus or squid? Deep breath, what does my stomach crave right now...

t_ch: 章鱼！
t_en: Octopus!

t_ch: 嗯，刚出锅、热乎乎的章鱼丸子，就像深海的金色宝箱一样。一口下去绵软咸鲜，而「宝藏」的弹牙口感更增添了一份惊喜……
t_en: Hmm... fresh out of the pan, this piping hot squid doughballs is like a golden treasure chest from the deep sea. It's soft and savory at first bite, while the chewy "treasure" inside offers an extra delightful surprise...

t_ch: 鱿鱼！
t_en: Squid!

t_ch: 说到鱿鱼，就是烤鱿鱼，重点在于食材新不新鲜，酱料如何调配。就像这里的老烧烤摊，他们的秘制酱料咸中带辣，香而不腻，让人食指大动……
t_en: If it's squid, then it's gotta be grilled. The secret's in the fresh ingredients and mixed sauce. Take this old BBQ stand right here. Their secret sauce has this perfect balance of salty and spicy that is rich but never greasy. It makes your mouth water just smelling it...

t_ch: （默默走开）
t_en: (Silently leave)

t_ch: 我决定了，各买五份。谢谢你的建议，年轻人。
t_en: I've made up my mind. Five orders of each. Thanks for the advice, young one.

t_ch: 你给我说饿了。
t_en: Now you're making me hungry.

t_ch: 哈哈，这是我的荣幸。如果想就近吃一顿，我推荐「多星籍餐厅」，能吃到其他世界的特色菜，尤其是「香炸云排」，酥脆得恰到好处。
t_en: Haha, I'll take that as a compliment. If you're looking for a good place nearby, try "Galactic Cuisine." They've got specialties from all across the galaxy. You won't go wrong with the "Cloud Cutlet." It's crispy, crunchy, and absolutely perfect.

t_ch: 这样吃健康吗？
t_en: Is that healthy?

t_ch: 哈哈，去它的健康饮食。我能大口吃着美食，不就证明我很健康吗！
t_en: Haha, to hell with healthy eating! If I can stuff my face with delicious food, that's proof enough I'm healthy!

t_ch: 听听五十岁大叔的人生建议吧——「人与美食的相遇总是很短暂。所以只要今天吃得下，就绝不要剩到明天。」
t_en: Take it from a guy in his fifties: "The encounter between you and delicacies is always fleeting. So if you can eat it today, never save it for tomorrow."

t_ch: 这么说来，大叔我作为「美食博主」的直播副业，已经赚得比本职还多了。
t_en: Come to think of it, my side gig as a "food influencer" is now making more than my actual job.

t_ch: 也许真的可以考虑旅游局的提案，担任形象大使，专职四处探店？
t_en: Maybe I should really consider the tourism agency's proposal to be the brand ambassador and make a career out of exploring different food?

t_ch: 还有这种好事？
t_en: There's an offer like that?

t_ch: 他们说我的吃相很好。每次我开播，都会给当地带来一个月的营收增长……
t_en: They said my eating face looks great on camera. Every time I stream, local restaurants see a whole month's worth of growth...

t_ch: 会束手束脚的吧。
t_en: Wouldn't that tie you down, though?

t_ch: 有道理…我还想趁下次出差，去罗浮仙舟尝尝那道出名的「苏打豆汁儿」呢。
t_en: Fair point... I still wanna try that legendary mung bean soda next time I'm on business at the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 算了，当务之急还是享受今日的美食！要是发现好吃的店，记得也跟大叔我说一声啊！
t_en: Forget it, now let's just focus on enjoying today's food! If you find any good restaurants, please do let me know!

t_ch: 这是很严肃的问题，是我们气氛组赖以生存下去的红线！
t_en: This is a serious matter. it's the bottom line that our Hype Squad's survival depends on!

t_ch: 我作为老大，一直在强调：「辨别！辨别！」——我们一定要为不同的人精准送去他们最需要的祝福，才能平安得到他们的关注和愿力！
t_en: As the boss, I've always emphasized: "Discern! Discern!" We must precisely deliver the most needed blessings to different people if we want to safely gain their attention and Wishpower!

t_ch: 是，芋头二当家！
t_en: Yes, Underboss Taro!

t_ch: （听听他们在聊什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 既然有新人加入，我就把咱们的「气氛组生存技巧」再讲解一次。
t_en: Since we have a newbie joining us, let me go over one more time our Hype Squad Survival Guide.

t_ch: 就算是做气氛组也要有职业道德。咱们最核心的职业素养就是具备鹰一样的敏锐和手术刀般的精确，在纷乱的地铁口，也绝对不能认错人！
t_en: Even as a Hype Squad member, you gotta have professional ethics. Our core quality is having an eagle's sharp eye and a surgeon's precision. That is, we absolutely can't mistake someone's identity in the chaos of a subway entrance!

t_ch: 注意听，一个丧眉耷眼、眼冒金星，走一步晃三步的人下午回家，他会是谁？
t_en: Listen up, a guy with droopy eyes, dizzy and dazed, and stumbling like he's about to fall over on his way home one afternoon, who do you think that'll be?

t_ch: 是上班族！
t_en: It's a salaryman!

t_ch: 错！咱们从来不给下班的上班族喊口号——我们又不加班！遇不见他们的！
t_en: Wrong! We never chant slogans at salarymen going home. We don't work overtime! We never run into them anyway!

t_ch: 记住，他是个学生，而且刚刚考了零鸭蛋，走一步晃三步是不想回家！
t_en: Remember, he's a student who just flunked an exam. If he's staggering with every step, it's because he doesn't want to go home!

t_ch: 明、明白了…加班的上班族和考试失败的学生，不也差不多嘛？
t_en: I—I get it... Salarymen doing overtime and students failing exams, they're pretty much in the same boat, right?

t_ch: 你这家伙不要碎碎念！虽然我们幻造种聚集起来做气氛组，求的就是那点关注和愿力，但一行就要有一行的规矩！
t_en: Stop your yapping! Sure, we imagenae gather to hype up the crowd for attention and Wishpower, but there are rules to follow in this line of work!

t_ch: 你们是不是觉得我芋头二当家特别麻烦，总喜欢强调这些无关紧要的细节？不，你们错了！让我给你们讲个故事吧！
t_en: You think I, Underboss Taro, am a pain in the neck, always harping on about these trivial details? No, you've got it all wrong! Let me tell you a story!

t_ch: 遥想我刚入行那年，大当家就草拟出了那些振聋发聩的口号，从上班族到无所事事者，甚至帮派分子都受到过他的鼓励！
t_en: Back in my early days, the boss came up with those mind-blowing slogans that inspired everyone from office workers to slackers, and even gang members!

t_ch: 但大家知道为什么现在我成老大了吗？
t_en: But do you all know why I'm the boss now?

t_ch: 因为那个最后的下午，他对着一个凶神恶煞的欧克高呼：「今天也要加油做个帮派分子啊！朝着二相乐园的治安挥舞拳头吧！」
t_en: Because on that final afternoon, he shouted at a fierce-looking orch: "Let's keep being a gang member today too! Let's raise our fists against the peace of Planarcadia!"

t_ch: 然后他就被一位蹲守已久的欧克便衣治安官拖走了。拘了七天，出来后就金盆洗手了。
t_en: Then he got dragged away by a terrifying orch undercover security officer. After seven days in detention, he came out with his spirit broken and swore off the life for good.

t_ch: 啊，每每想到这个沉重的故事，我的心还是会抽痛！各位，一定要记住这个教训——绝对不能认错别人的身份！
t_en: Ah, every time I think about this heavy story, my heart still aches! Everyone, remember this lesson and never ever mistake someone's identity!

t_ch: 你们应该来应援一下我！
t_en: You should come cheer me on!

t_ch: 我们气氛组会绝不认错别人的身份，想要应援的话您得告诉我们——您是谁？
t_en: The Hype Squad never misidentifies anyone. If you want our support, you'll have to tell us: Who are you?

t_ch: 我是还在读书的学生！
t_en: I'm a student still in school!

t_ch: 读书好啊！
t_en: A student! Excellent!

t_ch: 您每次来到这里，我们气氛组都会鼓励您好好读书的！
t_en: Whenever you come, the Hype Squad is here to cheer you on to hit the books!

t_ch: 我是进入社会的打工人！
t_en: I'm just another corporate slave in the rat race!

t_ch: 工作好啊！
t_en: Work is nice!

t_ch: 您每次来到这里，我们气氛组都会鼓励您加油工作的！
t_en: Whenever you come, the Hype Squad is here to cheer you on with your work!

t_ch: 我是…我是二维市的废物…
t_en: I'm... I'm just a loser in Duomension City...

t_ch: 废物？不对，您是在享受生活！
t_en: A loser? No way, you're living your best life!

t_ch: 您放心，就算您的生活方式不被人理解，我们气氛组都会支持您的！
t_en: Don't worry! Even if others don't understand your lifestyle, the Hype Squad will always have your back!

t_ch: 问别人之前，先说自己是谁。
t_en: Introduce yourself before asking others who they are.
...Huh?

t_ch: 行不更名，坐不改姓，我是气氛组的芋头二当家。请多指教！
t_en: True to my name and reputation, I'm Taro, the Underboss of the Hype Squad. Glad to know you!

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 市民您好，您已违反「二相乐园交通法」第五章十二条，在机动车道上超速行驶。
t_en: Greetings, citizen. You have violated Article <unbreak>12</unbreak>, Chapter 5 of the Planarcadia Traffic Law for speeding on the motorway.

t_ch: 鉴于初犯且情形轻微，您有一次机会，可以通过完成答题而从轻处罚。
t_en: Given that this is your first offense and a minor case, you have one chance to receive a lighter punishment by completing a questionnaire.

t_ch: （听听他们在说什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 真倒霉，刚提车想潇洒一把，就碰上「铁面的道穷司」…你可说好了，答个题就放我走。
t_en: Just my luck! I just got my car and wanted to get some fun, and I run into the "Down-Bad Jones"... You promised to let me go if I'd answer the questionnaire.

t_ch: 如果您在十个问题中正确回答八问，将不会进行行政拘留，仅会处以罚金。请听题：
t_en: If you answer eight out of ten questions correctly, you will not face administrative detention, only a fine. Please listen to the questions:

t_ch: 为何多数治安官不会选择追逐混混，直到将其逮捕？
t_en: Why don't most security officers choose to chase down the thugs until they're caught?

t_ch: 因为他们追不上！开玩笑的…是治安官也怕违法吧？
t_en: Because they can't catch up! Just kidding... The security officers are also afraid of breaking the law, I guess?

t_ch: 错误，你开再快我也追得上。而且我们拥有一定的执法豁免权，放弃追捕是避免危害公共安全。
t_en: Wrong, no matter how fast you go, I can still catch up. Besides, we have certain law enforcement immunity, and abandoning pursuit is about avoiding risks to public safety.

t_ch: 下一问，治安官应该如何灵活应对市民提出的「手下留情」请求？
t_en: Next one: How should a security officer flexibly handle citizens' requests to go easy on them?

t_ch: 适当减轻罚额？
t_en: Maybe reduce the penalty a bit?

t_ch: 错误，那是渎职行为——但我们会在开罚单的时候写字写慢点。
t_en: Wrong, that would be dereliction of duty, but we'll write the ticket nice and slow.

t_ch: 下一问，机铠以<unbreak>50</unbreak>公里/系统时的速度在正向车道行驶，该段道路有限速<unbreak>60</unbreak>公里的指示牌，请问这一场景中是否存在违规行为？
t_en: Moving along: A mechatron is traveling at <unbreak>50</unbreak> kilometers per system hour in the forward lane. There's a speed limit sign showing <unbreak>60</unbreak> kilometers. Is there any violation in this scenario?

t_ch: 呃…没有？
t_en: Uh... nope?

t_ch: 错误，机铠整体属于幻造种，应该走非机动车道。您已用光错误次数，请在此等待转运。
t_en: Wrong, mechatrons are classified as imagenae and should use the non-motorized lane. You've exhausted your chances. Please wait here for transfer.

t_ch: 不要啊——我不想蹲局子啊——
t_en: No way! I don't wanna end up in the slammer!

t_ch: 路过的各位市民，为了您和他人的安全，请遵守交通规则。祝您生活愉快。
t_en: Dear citizens passing by, for your and other people's safety, please follow traffic regulations. Have a pleasant day.

t_ch: （不听了）
t_en: (Stop listening)

t_ch: 这位路过的宝子，用你可爱的小手点个关注呗~要不要再动动手指刷个愿宝？可以解锁全新舞蹈哦~
t_en: Hey there cutie~ Mind using those adorable fingers to give us a follow? And while you're at it, why not tap that Wishbae button? You'll unlock a brand new dance routine~

t_ch: 不刷的话麻烦让一让，别挡着其他路过的宝宝打赏愿力好吗？
t_en: If you're not donating, could you please step aside? Don't block other sweeties trying to give their Wishpower, okay?

t_ch: 集中意念——打赏愿力！
t_en: Focus your mind. Unleash the Wishpower!

t_ch: 咦，你还真的打赏愿宝呀！
t_en: Whoa, you actually tipped Wishbae!

t_ch: 不对，愿力还在增加…是心愿火箭…不，是豪华愿景摩天轮…不，是愿满天成梦幻嘉年华！
t_en: No wait, the Wishpower is still rising... It's a Wish Rocket... Hold on, it's a Deluxe Wish Ferris Wheel... No way, it's THE Dream Carnival of Fulfilled Wishes!

t_ch: 呼，至少这阵子不必担心消失，可以稍微歇歇了。老板大气！
t_en: Phew, at least I don't have to worry about disappearing for a while. I can finally catch a break. Thank you for being so generous!

t_ch: 老板出手这么阔绰，真是让蒲儿心里砰砰直跳呢。老板想看什么舞吗，还是想和蒲儿聊聊天呢？
t_en: I gotta say, you're so generous it makes my heart flutter! Would you like to see me dance, or would you prefer to chat with me?

t_ch: 态度大转弯了！
t_en: What a complete <unbreak>180</unbreak>!

t_ch: 翻脸比翻书还快。
t_en: You changed your attitude in the blink of an eye.

t_ch: 别生气嘛老板，我承认刚才说话有点大声了。像我们轻脚这种满大街都是的幻造种，也只有靠这样吸引路人的目光，才能得到一点点愿力了。
t_en: Please don't be mad at me. I admit I was a bit loud earlier. As a lightpaw, one of those dime-a-dozen imagenae, we can only get a tiny bit of Wishpower by catching people's attention like this.

t_ch: 去掉直播成本，再去掉平台抽成，即便一天跳到晚，也只是勉强足够维持存在而已。一旦停下来，没人喜欢我，甚至没人讨厌我…我就消失了。
t_en: After deducting streaming costs and platform fees, even if I dance from dawn till dusk, I'm barely making enough to live. Once I stop, no one will like me, not even hate me... and I'll just disappear.

t_ch: 老板老板，你舍得让蒲儿消失吗？
t_en: You wouldn't let me disappear, would you?

t_ch: 那我再多给你传输些愿力！
t_en: Then I'll transmit more Wishpower to you!

t_ch: 老板你最好了！咦，不过，你身上似乎没有新鲜的愿力了哦？说什么要给蒲儿愿力，只是说说而已吗？
t_en: You're the best! Huh, but... looks like you don't have any fresh Wishpower on you? All that talk about giving me Wishpower... Was that just empty promises?

t_ch: 救急不救穷，这也没办法。
t_en: Emergency care won't fix being broke. That's just how it is.

t_ch: 我就知道…你们为了有人陪伴创造了轻脚，一旦腻了就把我们遗弃在街头…有兴趣时来哄一哄，厌倦了就看也不看我们一眼……
t_en: I knew it... You created lightpaw just to have company, then abandoned us on the streets once you got bored... You pamper us when you feel like it, and ignore us completely when you're tired of us...

t_ch: 也没关系…反正但凡一有充裕的愿力，蒲儿都会把它花掉的。漂亮衣裳啦，名牌香水啦，真皮包包啦…想要我的愿力？通通拿去好了。
t_en: It's fine though... whenever there's plenty of Wishpower, I will spend it all anyway. Designer clothes, fancy perfumes, genuine leather bags... Want my Wishpower? Take it all, be my guest.

t_ch: 反正，也不知道明天自己还会不会在这里……
t_en: Well, who knows if I'll even still be here tomorrow...

t_ch: 让开就让开。
t_en: Alright, I'll step aside.

t_ch: 这就对了，我们可不像你们有用不完的时间。路过的宝子们，用你可爱的小手点个关注吧，求求啦~
t_en: That's more like it! Unlike you folks, we don't have all the time in the world. Hey there passersby, give us a follow with those adorable little fingers of yours, pretty please~

t_ch: 哇！年轻美丽的小姐，您超群的气质我隔着一条街就嗅…就发现了。
t_en: Wow! Beautiful young lady, your exceptional aura... I could sniff... I mean, I could notice it from all the way across the street!

t_ch: ——你在闻什么！
t_en: ...What are you sniffing at!

t_ch: 别误会，我是来自寰宇知名大事务所「超星娱乐」的星探尾巴长。这是我的名片。怎么样，有兴趣加入我们事务所，逐梦演艺圈吗？
t_en: Don't get me wrong, I'm Longtail, a talent scout from "Superstar Entertainment," a renowned entertainment company across the universe. Here's my business card. So, what do you say? Interested in joining our agency and pursuing your dreams in show business?

t_ch: （听听他们在聊什么）
t_en: (Listen to what they are saying)

t_ch: 正常星探会在这闻来闻去吗？管好你的鼻子！
t_en: Would a normal talent scout be sniffing around like that? Keep your nose to yourself!

t_ch: 美丽的小姐，或许您有听说过这样一句话——「一流的星探都具备对时尚最敏锐的嗅觉」，而我的鼻子便是如此。
t_en: My lovely lady, perhaps you've heard this saying, "The best talent scouts have the keenest nose for fashion," and that's just how my nose is.
